title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair
description: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. Learners cumulatively produce a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.
Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
